source.transliteration: IVTFF/EVA (ZL file as downloaded by scripts/build_pages.py)

safe_complex_herbal_blend: gentle spices (e.g., 1 g cinnamon + 1 g clove) or a commercial herbal tea blend

page_summary_procedural.disclaimer: IMPORTANT: this is NOT a real or validated translation of the Voynich Manuscript. It is a speculative/procedural model that interprets EVA using a user-defined grammar to generate experimental recipes using safe, known edible substitutes.
page_summary_procedural.expected_result: A mild, aromatic herbal ferment, low-to-medium intensity depending on dose level.
risks: Never use unidentified Voynich plants directly; only use known edible substitutes.
Do not consume if you see mold, smell rot, notice abnormal sliminess, or taste something clearly foul.
Overpressure/bottle-bomb risk: do not bottle before stable; prefer an airlock and refrigeration.
Avoid if pregnant/breastfeeding, for minors, or with medical conditions; consult a professional.
No medical claims: this is an experimental beverage.
recommended_adjustments: If too bitter (leafy profile), halve the herbs or shorten steep/maceration time.
If too sweet, extend fermentation or reduce sugar by 25–50%.
For a non-alcoholic version, omit yeast and keep refrigerated as an infusion (not fermented).

parsing.interpretation: add secondary herb (safe substitute) → mix / transfer → start fermentation (yeast)

process: Sanitize the jar/fermenter and utensils.
Base: combine 0.5 L water with 12 g sugar or honey.
Infusion: use hot (not boiling) water, then let it cool before adding yeast.
Add main plant: chamomile (safe default substitute) (~2 g dried).
Add secondary herb: mint (~2 g dried).
Pitch yeast: 1 g (ideally cider/beer yeast).
Ferment with an airlock: 2–4 days (guided by iin/aiin markers).
Strain/rack (if very solid-heavy) and cold-crash 24 h.
Bottle only when activity clearly slows; refrigerate. Avoid overpressure.
recipe.expected_result: A mild, aromatic herbal ferment, low-to-medium intensity depending on dose level.
risks: Never use unidentified Voynich plants directly; only use known edible substitutes.
Do not consume if you see mold, smell rot, notice abnormal sliminess, or taste something clearly foul.
Overpressure/bottle-bomb risk: do not bottle before stable; prefer an airlock and refrigeration.
Avoid if pregnant/breastfeeding, for minors, or with medical conditions; consult a professional.
No medical claims: this is an experimental beverage.
recommended_adjustments: If too bitter (leafy profile), halve the herbs or shorten steep/maceration time.
If too sweet, extend fermentation or reduce sugar by 25–50%.
For a non-alcoholic version, omit yeast and keep refrigerated as an infusion (not fermented).

process: Sanitize the jar/fermenter and utensils.
Base: combine 0.5 L water with 50 g sugar or honey.
Apply gentle heat: simmer 10–15 min, then cool to <30°C before adding yeast.
Add main plant: chamomile (safe default substitute) (~10 g dried).
Add secondary herb: mint (~5 g dried).
Pitch yeast: 1 g (ideally cider/beer yeast).
Ferment with an airlock: 7–14 days (guided by iin/aiin markers).
Strain/rack (if very solid-heavy) and cold-crash 24 h.
Bottle only when activity clearly slows; refrigerate. Avoid overpressure.
recipe.expected_result: A mild, aromatic herbal ferment, low-to-medium intensity depending on dose level.
risks: Never use unidentified Voynich plants directly; only use known edible substitutes.
Do not consume if you see mold, smell rot, notice abnormal sliminess, or taste something clearly foul.
Overpressure/bottle-bomb risk: do not bottle before stable; prefer an airlock and refrigeration.
Avoid if pregnant/breastfeeding, for minors, or with medical conditions; consult a professional.
No medical claims: this is an experimental beverage.
recommended_adjustments: If too bitter (leafy profile), halve the herbs or shorten steep/maceration time.
If too sweet, extend fermentation or reduce sugar by 25–50%.
For a non-alcoholic version, omit yeast and keep refrigerated as an infusion (not fermented).
